steps.title: Strashed AIG: 5 ANDs, level 3
steps.narrative:
Starting circuit: f = ab + ad + cd, written as a 5-AND AIG. ABC's strash assigned IDs n6 = a&b, n7 = a&d, n9 = c&d; n8 = NOR(n6, n7) = ~(ab+ad); the f-AND is n8 & ~n9 with the output negated, giving f = ~n8 + n9 = (ab+ad) + cd.

Dashed edges = inverter on the wire.

steps.title: Pick a node to rewrite — start with f
steps.narrative: Abc_NtkRewrite visits every internal AND in topological order. We focus on f — that's where the trace's gain happens. The other four ANDs (n6, n7, n8, n9) will also be tried but won't yield gain.

panes.title: What rewrite tries to do
panes.body: For every 4-input cut at f, look up the cut's truth table in a precomputed library of NPN-canonical AIG subgraphs. Pick the variant that, after MFFC accounting, replaces the cone with fewer ANDs.

steps.title: Enumerate 4-input cuts at f
steps.narrative:
Abc_NodeGetCutsRecursive returns the cut chain at f. Cuts are computed bottom-up by merging fanin cut sets (Cut_NodeMerge); each is a set of leaves whose conjunction-cone covers f.

Rewrite skips cuts with < 4 leaves (rwrEva.c:96) — only the full 4-leaf cuts are evaluated.

panes.body: (n5 in this list refers to the same AND that the post-strash dump renames to n6; ABC's internal IDs depend on visit order.)

steps.title: Cut filter: reject cuts with > 2 fanout-1 leaves
steps.narrative:
Cuts whose leaves have fanout 1 represent leaves that are captive to the MFFC — their absence from the rest of the network means there's no shared structure to reuse. rwrEva.c:122..129 rejects cuts with strictly more than 2 such leaves.

For each candidate, count the leaves whose fanout count = 1.

steps.title: Try each surviving cut, score by gain
steps.narrative: For each surviving cut, compute the cut's truth table, look up its NPN class, evaluate the library's candidate subgraphs, and pick the best gain. The cut {a, c, d, n6} wins (per the actual ABC trace).

entries.Cone: { f, n8, n7, n9 } — 4 internal nodes

panes.body: Note that n6 (= a&b) is treated as a leaf even though it's an internal AND. Its sub-cone (the a&b AND) is "outside" the cut from rewrite's perspective and won't be touched.

steps.title: Apply boundary boost: leaf fanouts +1
steps.narrative: Before computing the MFFC, abcRewrite.c:138..148 increments each cut leaf's fanout count by 1 to protect them from the upcoming dereference. This guarantees the recursion stops at the cut.

steps.title: MFFC deref step 1: visit f
steps.narrative: Abc_NodeRefDeref(f, deref, label=1) begins. count = 1; mark f with the current TravId; deref f's two fanins next.

steps.title: MFFC deref step 2: f.fanin0 = n8 → fanout 1→0, recurse
steps.narrative: f's fanin0 is n8. Decrement its fanout count: 1 → 0. Zero ⇒ recurse into n8.

steps.title: MFFC deref step 3: n8.fanin0 = n6 → 2→1, no recurse
steps.narrative: n8's fanin0 is n6, which is a cut leaf. Its boundary-boosted fanout decrements 2 → 1. Not zero ⇒ recursion stops here. This is the boundary trick in action.

steps.title: MFFC deref step 4: n8.fanin1 = n7 → 1→0, recurse
steps.narrative: n8's other fanin is n7. Fanout 1 → 0 ⇒ recurse into n7. count=3, mark n7.

steps.title: MFFC deref step 5: n7's fanins are leaves a, d
steps.narrative: n7 = a & d. Both fanins are cut leaves. Their boosted counts decrement but stay positive — no further recursion.

steps.title: MFFC deref step 6: f.fanin1 = n9 → 1→0, recurse
steps.narrative: Back at f, deref the second fanin n9. Fanout 1 → 0 ⇒ recurse. count=4, mark n9. Its fanins c, d are cut leaves.

steps.title: MFFC complete: nNodesSaved = 4
steps.narrative:
Recursion done. MFFC = { f, n8, n7, n9 }; size 4. This matches the ABC trace's Save = 4.

(A re-reference pass walks the same path in reverse, restoring every fanout count. Then the boundary boost is stripped. The AIG is byte-identical to before, but the four MFFC nodes carry the current TravId.)

steps.title: Look up library candidates for NPN class 12
steps.narrative: Rwr_CutEvaluate (rwrEva.c:253) iterates the precomputed subgraph list p->vClasses[12]. Each candidate is a Dec_Graph_t — a small algebraic DAG over 4 leaves implementing the canonical truth table for class 12.

panes.title: Candidate subgraphs (illustrative)
panes.body: Class 12 includes several DAGs. ABC's library is built once at startup by Rwr_ManPrecompute; for class 12 the cheapest implementations have 3 internal ANDs over 4 leaves.

Candidate A: m1 = ~b · ~d ; m2 = a · ~m1 ; m3 = c · d ; result = ~(~m2 · ~m3)
Candidate B: m1 = a · b ; m2 = c · d ; m3 = ~m1 · ~(a·d) ; result = ~m3
... (more)
For each candidate, score with Dec_GraphToNetworkCount: how many AIG nodes would actually be added if we materialized this subgraph, given that strash hits and MFFC tags can let us skip nodes.

steps.title: Score candidates with Dec_GraphToNetworkCount
steps.narrative:
Dec_GraphToNetworkCount walks each candidate's internal nodes; for each, it asks Abc_AigAndLookup: does this AND already exist? If yes and the existing node is NOT in our MFFC, count as reuse. Otherwise count as add.

For our cut, no candidate's structure pre-exists (the relevant ANDs would be inside the MFFC, hence about to be deleted), so all 3 internal ANDs of the chosen candidate are counted as adds.

panes.body: Gain > 0 ⇒ accept (with default fUseZeros = 0; the -z flag would also accept gain == 0).

steps.title: Commit replacement: 5 ANDs → 4 ANDs
steps.narrative:
Dec_GraphUpdateNetwork materializes the 3 new ANDs over leaves {a, c, d, n6} and redirects f's PO to the new top node. The old MFFC ({f, n8, n7, n9}) becomes dangling and is cleaned up by reference counting.

The pre-existing n6 = a&b is untouched — that's exactly why it was kept as a cut leaf.

panes.body: Run write_blif on the result and you'll see m_or = ~b · ~d, m_and = a · ~m_or, m_cd = c · d, f = m_and + m_cd — exactly the factoring a(b+d) + cd = ab + ad + cd.
